title: woof JSONL event
description: One line of .woof/epics/E<N>/epic.jsonl (stage-level narrative) or .woof/epics/E<N>/dispatch.jsonl (subprocess events). Both streams share this schema; the event field discriminates. Per-event payload fields validate via the conditional rules in allOf. Cross-line invariants (subprocess_returned matches a prior subprocess_spawned by pid; gate_resolved follows gate_opened; status transitions are monotonic) are helper-enforced, not schema-enforced.

event.description: Event kind. Stage events (epic.jsonl): spark_created, current_epic_selected, discovery_*, definition_*, readiness_passed, readiness_gate_opened, readiness_gate_resolved, plan_*, breakdown_planned, work_unit_*, *_gate_*, gate_resolved, epic_completed, epic_abandoned, executor_result_recorded, check_run, work_unit_committed, transaction_manifest_verified. work_unit_abandoned records a work unit skipped via the abandon_work_unit gate verb; epic_abandoned is the graph-owned terminal marker the abandon_epic verb writes, consulted by next_node to return an abandoned-terminal outcome. Dispatch events (dispatch.jsonl): subprocess_*. Operational events (either): preflight_run, tracker_synced, tracker_sync_conflict, wf_lock_stale_removed, cartography_refreshed, token_usage. The github_synced and github_sync_conflict values are retained as legacy aliases so epic.jsonl logs written before the ADR-003 issue-tracker abstraction still validate.

epic_id.description: Epic this event belongs to (tracker-assigned epic identifier).

run_id.description: Durable run identity carried across dispatch events and immutable attempt artefacts.

work_unit_id.description: Work-unit identifier, local to the work-unit aggregate.

attempt_id.description: Immutable dispatch or review-cache reuse attempt identifier.

role.description: Semantic role under which a subprocess was dispatched.

harness.description: Interactive TUI harness profile used for dispatch. cld/cod/deepclaude are retained as legacy aliases.

config_role.description: Role entry read from .woof/agents.toml when it differs from the semantic requested role.

model_profile.description: Selected .woof/agents.toml model profile applied to this route.

profile_role.description: Role entry read from the selected model profile when it supplied route overrides.

route_key.description: Node group that selected this dispatch route (discovery, definition, planning, or execution). Present when the dispatch caller supplied a route_key; absent for direct woof dispatch invocations without --route-key.

effort.description: Resolved per-invocation reasoning effort for the subprocess route.

mcp.description: Resolved MCP server names for the subprocess route.

argv.description: Resolved harness launch argv. Prompt payloads delivered through tmux_harness prompt files are represented with <prompt:tmux-file> in durable events.

prompt_transport.description: How the full prompt body was delivered to the worker. stdin is retained for legacy audit logs.

exit_type.description: Supervised subprocess outcome classification. completed_lingering means the adapter emitted its terminal marker and Woof reaped a lingering process group; it is a successful dispatch classification.

timed_out.description: True when the supervised subprocess ended through the pre-terminal idle or wall-clock timeout clocks.

terminal_seen.description: True when the adapter-specific terminal marker was observed on stdout.

hostname.description: Host that observed or emitted the event.

reason.description: Free-form reason. Used by subprocess_killed (timeout, manual_cancel, ...). Gate resolution events use the structured 'decision' field instead.

decision.description: Gate resolution decision. approve = proceed. approve_with_reason = record audited readiness approval so the unchanged contract advances to planning without re-running the readiness node (readiness_gate only). revise_epic_contract = re-enter Stage 2. revise_plan = re-enter Stage 3. revise_work_unit_scope = edit current work unit's paths or satisfies. abandon_work_unit = skip and continue. abandon_epic = mark incomplete + close the tracker epic with a note. keep_local, accept_remote, and hand_merge resolve tracker sync-conflict gates.

claude_transcript_path.description: Portable home-relative reference to Claude Code's native project transcript.

tmux_session.description: tmux_harness session name for the logical dispatch.

worker_session_id.description: Structured worker session identifier reported by the harness payload.

worker_session_path.description: Structured worker session path reported by the harness payload.

worker_session_transcript_path.description: Structured worker transcript path reported by the harness payload.

worker_session_thread_id.description: Structured worker thread id reported by the harness payload.

codex_audit_path.description: Path under .woof/epics/E<N>/audit/ where Codex prompt+output+meta were tee'd.

manifest.description: Transaction manifest payload recorded after graph-owned manifest verification.

paths.description: Repo-relative files or artefacts associated with the event.

bucket.description: Stage-1 discovery bucket. research/thinking/ideate are the headless producer buckets; brainstorm is the interactive Stage-0 bucket written by `woof brainstorm`. Used by discovery_bucket_explored events.

tokens_in.description: Prompt tokens. Used by token_usage events and post-subprocess summaries.

artefacts_loaded.description: Repo-relative artefact references explicitly loaded or embedded into the dispatched prompt. Useful for audit reconstruction and token-budget post-mortems.

prompt_bytes.description: UTF-8 byte count of the prompt sent to the dispatch adapter.

prompt_hash.description: SHA-256 of the prompt sent to the dispatch adapter.

prompt_version.description: Prompt version key used for review cache reuse. The default is sha256:<prompt_hash>.

diff_hash.description: SHA-256 of the staged binary diff that the reviewer was asked to inspect.

review_cache_key.description: Stable key over work_unit_id, diff_hash, and prompt_version for reviewer verdict reuse.

review_cache_hit.description: True when the reviewer verdict and critique artefact were reused from the review cache.

review_instability_path.description: Repo-relative path to the review-instability record for conflicting verdicts over the same review inputs.

artefact_bytes.description: Total byte count of repo-relative artefacts listed in artefacts_loaded at dispatch time.

output_bytes.description: UTF-8 byte count of captured subprocess stdout.

stderr_bytes.description: UTF-8 byte count of captured subprocess stderr.

command_count.description: Completed Codex command_execution tool calls observed in codex exec --json output.

error_signature.description: Normalised signature of subprocess error output on subprocess_returned. Volatile absolute/relative paths, line/column spans, ISO-8601 timestamps, UUIDs, and excess whitespace are stripped; standalone integers are preserved; truncated to 256 characters. Absent when stderr is empty.

rate_limit.description: Rate-limit classification on subprocess_returned. Present only when the adapter stdout/stderr signals rate-limiting (HTTP 429, 'too many requests', 'overloaded', etc.); absent otherwise.

verdict.description: Structured dispatch verdict reported by the worker payload.

evidence.description: Structured evidence reported by the worker payload.

result_artefacts.description: Structured artefact references reported by the worker payload.

head_before.description: Git HEAD short SHA read immediately before the supervised subprocess was spawned. Absent when HEAD is unreadable (e.g. empty repo or detached without commits).

head_after.description: Git HEAD short SHA read immediately after the supervised subprocess returned.

branch_before.description: Git branch name read immediately before the supervised subprocess was spawned. Absent when in detached HEAD state or unreadable.

branch_after.description: Git branch name read immediately after the supervised subprocess returned.

runtime_policy.description: Runtime action-safety model declared for a dispatched subprocess. Trusted-local mode means Woof does not add runtime constraints and relies on commit-safety boundaries before changes land.

woof_runtime_constraints.description: Empty in trusted-local mode; Woof adds no sandbox, command allow-list, writable-path, network, or MCP runtime constraints.
